page: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.4 GHz, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 256 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference, with prompt processing and text generation speeds for 135M, 7B, and 70B models placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Geekbench single-core execution and image processing also achieve top-tier ratings. Strong performance in the top 25% is observed in uncached memory bandwidth read/write operations, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Conversely, stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench ray tracing fall within the average middle 50% range.

From a resource density perspective, the g7e.8xlarge offers a high-capacity GPU memory allocation of 96 GB VRAM alongside 256 GB of system RAM, representing a specialized hardware profile for memory-heavy graphics and AI workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation and advanced instruction sets, such as Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, enhance its capability for localized machine learning tasks. However, general CPU-bound compute tasks and database operations perform at average levels relative to the instance's high-tier hardware class. This instance is optimized for LLM inference, image processing, and graphics-intensive applications requiring substantial VRAM and high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a local 1900 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, backed by 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference across small, medium, and large models, as well as Geekbench image processing. Uncached memory bandwidth and multi-core decompression benchmarks rank in the top 25%, while standard CPU-bound stress tests and ray tracing perform in the average tier. This hardware profile is engineered for large language model inference, image processing, and memory-intensive graphics workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, 96 GB of Blackwell VRAM, and high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM for memory-heavy graphics and AI tasks
Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and Geekbench image processing benchmarks
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Optimized for large language model inference, image processing, and graphics workloads
